text.title: Risk Register Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the risk-register methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every risk has exactly one named individual as owner; 'the team' is not valid.
rule.rationale: Risks without a single owner are observed, not managed.

rule.statement: Every trigger must be an observable signal — a metric, log event, or date — not a subjective phrase.
rule.rationale: Unobservable triggers cannot be monitored; risks activate without warning.

rule.statement: Every Accept strategy entry must include contingency_amount or contingency_plan.
rule.rationale: Accept without a contingency is denial dressed as a strategy.

rule.statement: Risk score must equal probability_level × impact_level; stored score must match computed value.
rule.rationale: Score integrity is required for heatmap accuracy and prioritisation correctness.

rule.statement: Include at least one opportunity (positive risk) for every ten threats.
rule.rationale: Sponsor approval rates rise when upside is visible; LLMs drop opportunities by default.

rule.statement: Calibrate the impact scale with a project-specific reference point before scoring.
rule.rationale: Uncalibrated scales produce inconsistent scores across assessors and over time.

rule.statement: Risk-driven contingency (sum of P × cost_impact per open risk) must replace flat-percentage reserves for any project with an active register.
rule.rationale: Flat reserves systematically over- or under-fund based on risk profile.

list.item: Pure-Scrum teams already tracking risks as backlog impediments
Tasks under 1 week with a single owner
Pre-discovery R&D where uncertainty is the work — use a learning log
Risks already covered by a separate risk system (GDPR, security)
